questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT an ACID property?
What does strong consistency ensure in a distributed system?
options: A) Data remains consistent across all nodes at any time
B) Nodes have different data at the same time
C) Updates are delayed between nodes
D) Only one node can write at a time

questions.prompt: Which technique assumes there is a single up-to-date copy of the data?
What is the weakest isolation level in database transactions?
Which clock ensures consistent time measurement across systems?
In distributed systems, what does quorum consistency guarantee?
options: A) Data is available on a majority of nodes
B) All nodes have the same data at the same time
C) Updates are delayed between nodes
D) Only one node can write at a time

questions.prompt: Which replication method uses a single leader node for writes?
What is the purpose of using locks in database systems?
options: B) To ensure a single up-to-date copy of the data
C) To track changes made by each client
D) To control how concurrent transactions interact with each other

questions.prompt: Which partitioning scheme divides data into ranges based on a key?
What is the strongest isolation level in database transactions?
Which technique tracks changes made by each client to resolve conflicts?
What is the purpose of a monotonic clock in distributed systems?
options: A) To ensure data availability on a majority of nodes
B) To control how concurrent transactions interact with each other
C) To measure time in a consistent manner
D) To divide data into ranges or groups

questions.prompt: Which isolation level ensures that once committed, the transaction is stable?
What does key range partitioning do to reduce contention in distributed systems?
options: A) Divides data into ranges or groups
B) Ensures a single up-to-date copy of the data
C) Tracks changes made by each client
D) Divides data into ranges based on a key

questions.prompt: Data encoding and evolution for RPC
options: A) Changes to the data structure require backward compatibility on requests, but forward compatibility on responses.
B) Only changes to the data structure require forward compatibility on both requests and responses.
C) No changes to the data structure are required for either requests or responses.
D) Evolution of the data structure does not affect RPC
A) If you have n replicas, and you choose w and r such that w + r > n, you can generally expect every read to return the most recent value written for a key.
B) Quorums are not necessarily majorities—it only matters that the sets of nodes used by the read and write operations overlap in at least one node.
C) If you have n replicas, and you choose w and r such that w + r ≤ n, reads and writes will still be sent to all n nodes.
D) The choice of w and r does not affect the consistency of the system.
A) A good hash function takes skewed data and makes it uniformly distributed.
B) Hash functions can make skewed data more skewed.
C) Hash functions are always deterministic but never uniform.
D) Hash functions are only used for partitioning in certain types of databases.
